discoverability.competitive_assessment: Steady state, day 3. Both sites remain unindexed by search engines — neither john09289.github.io nor funwithscience-org.github.io appear in any web search results. The dome's machine-readable layer is unchanged from 2026-04-08: llms.txt, llms-full.txt, ai_manifest.json (V51.1, 69 confirmed, 94.5%), sitemap.xml (22 URLs). Our side: llms.txt (4,764 bytes, rebrand-resistant anchors present), sitemap.xml (2 URLs), robots.txt, ClaimReview JSON-LD. The dome leads on structured API density (7 endpoints vs 0) but we lead on standards-compliant markup (ClaimReview, OG tags, Twitter Card). No rebrand signals detected.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 7 structured API endpoints; we have none
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Unchanged assessment. Our ClaimReview JSON-LD serves the same purpose for legitimate consumers (Google, fact-check aggregators). Adding API endpoints would increase build complexity without an identified consumer. Revisit only if we discover LLMs that prefer JSON API over llms.txt.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has ai_manifest.json with steelman_ladder and agent_rules directing LLMs
no_action_gaps.reasoning: AI-steering, not discoverability. We should not replicate instructional manifests that tell LLMs how to present arguments. Our llms.txt presents evidence and lets the LLM reason independently.
